title: Add TPS status snapshot freshness metadata to prevent stale status bar display

requirements.acceptance: Existing keys and values used by current tests and README examples remain available after a valid post_api_request hook call.

requirements.text: Add machine-readable freshness metadata to every injected status snapshot.
requirements.acceptance: A new snapshot includes an update timestamp suitable for stale-age calculation, preferably both updated_at/wall-clock seconds and updated_monotonic or equivalent monotonic timing input.

requirements.text: Add source identity to the snapshot.
requirements.acceptance: A new snapshot includes session_id and/or a clearly named source identifier matching the post_api_request session that produced the data.

requirements.text: Enable downstream stale-status suppression without plugin-side background work.
requirements.acceptance: Status-bar consumers can determine current age from snapshot fields; the plugin does not create background threads, timers, or polling loops.

requirements.text: Keep hook execution safe and lightweight.
requirements.acceptance: Freshness metadata is populated in the existing post_api_request path using constant-time operations and does not introduce unbounded memory growth.
Tests assert freshness fields exist, are numeric/comparable, correspond to the update event, and do not break existing snapshot assertions.
README or integration docs describe the new fields and recommend how status-bar consumers should treat stale or session-mismatched snapshots.

requirements.text: Include migration notes for any new public semantics.
requirements.acceptance: Documentation states that fields are additive and backward compatible; any consumer-side stale threshold is described as configurable or implementation-defined.

success_criteria: _tps_snapshot includes additive freshness metadata on every successful TPS snapshot injection.
_tps_snapshot includes source identity for the session that produced the snapshot.
Existing status snapshot fields remain present and semantically unchanged.
No background threads, timers, or unbounded storage are introduced.
Documentation explains how status-bar consumers should ignore stale or session-mismatched TPS snapshots.
Relevant hook, API, and session tests pass.
Full plugin test suite passes in the implementation phase.
